q: She know the truth, she would be devastated.
a: If she knew the truth, she would be devastated.

q: I would travel around the world, I have enough money.
a: If I had enough money, I would travel around the world.

q: He is taller, he could become a professional basketball player.
a: If he were taller, he could become a professional basketball player.

q: You were not so tired, you went to bed earlier.
a: If you were not so tired, you would go to bed earlier.

q: They offer me the job, I would accept it immediately.
a: If they offered me the job, I would accept it immediately.

q: A bike in my possession would mean daily rides.
a: If I had a bike, I would ride it daily.

q: Her presence would mean assistance with our homework.
a: If she were here, she would help us with our homework.

q: Winning the lottery would lead us to travel around the world.
a: If we won the lottery, we would travel around the world.

q: More study from him would result in passing his exams.
a: If he studied more, he would pass his exams.

q: Rain tomorrow means staying indoors.
a: If it rained tomorrow, we would stay indoors.

q: Kathy is shy. That's why she doesn't have many friends.
a: If Kathy were not shy, she would have many friends.

q: He doesn't know her address, so he can't write to her.
a: If he knew her address, he could write to her.

q: I don't have enough money. I can't buy that laptop.
a: If I had enough money, I could buy that laptop.

q: They don't have a car. They can't drive to the beach.
a: If they had a car, they could drive to the beach.

q: She doesn't find his number, so she doesn't call him.
a: If she found his number, she would call him.

q: The boy doesn't study hard. He doesn't get good marks.
a: If the boy studied hard, he would get good marks.

q: We don't live near the school, so we can't walk there.
a: If we lived near the school, we could walk there.

q: He doesn't eat vegetables, so he's not healthy.
a: If he ate vegetables, he would be healthy.

q: I don't speak French, so I can't work in Paris.
a: If I spoke French, I could work in Paris.

q: She doesn't have time, so she doesn't go on holiday.
a: If she had time, she would go on holiday.

q: They don't invite him because they don't like him.
a: If they liked him, they would invite him.

q: He doesn't work harder, so he doesn't get promoted.
a: If he worked harder, he would get promoted.

q: We don't know the answer, so we don't raise our hands.
a: If we knew the answer, we would raise our hands.

q: He doesn't save money, so he can't buy a car.
a: If he saved money, he could buy a car.

q: The child doesn't sleep early, so he feels tired.
a: If the child slept early, he wouldn't feel tired.

q: I don't own a house, so I pay rent.
a: If I owned a house, I wouldn't pay rent.

q: She doesn't learn how to cook, so she always eats out.
a: If she learned how to cook, she wouldn't always eat out.

q: He doesn't take care of his bike, so it breaks down.
a: If he took care of his bike, it wouldn't break down.

q: I don't have a passport, so I can't travel abroad.
a: If I had a passport, I could travel abroad.

q: They don't watch the news, so they don't know what's happening.
a: If they watched the news, they would know what's happening.
